title: Cross-Chat Surface Notification
statement: When chat A acts on a surface that chat B has planned work against (visible in chat B's deliverable specs on chat B's repo), chat A's action MUST produce a substrate artifact discoverable by chat B. Same-repo same-branch overlap is covered by RULE-PIPELINE-AMENDMENT-SUBSTRATE-RECHECK-1; this rule extends to cross-repo, cross-chat coordination where neither chat owns the other's substrate.
rationale: Cross-chat coordination failure mode surfaced 2026-06-01: SR chat archived venture-summary.schema.json during D-1 work while EB chat was authoring Wave 2 deliverable specs (d-2026-06-02-deprecation-header-convention, d-2026-06-04-portfolio-venture-summary-archival) against the active canonical path. EB had no signal until execution-time HALT — caught only because EB-agent surfaced the conflict in alignment prompt to SR. Without a notification surface, cross-chat acts produce silent invalidation of the other chat's planned work.

authority.author: EB-agent (chat), L1; relayed and schematized by SR chat (Claude L1)

human-loop.gate: Acting chat posts a direct message (Slack channel or canvas) to the affected chat before authoring the dispatch that touches the cross-chat surface. Post must include: the substrate being touched, the affected chat's known planned work, and the proposed action's expected impact.
human-loop.authority: Acting chat agent (L1) per Erik L4 approval of the underlying action
human-loop.automationPath: D-COMMS-1 (sequence position 6) builds the #luhtech-agents-comms Slack channel + GHA broadcast workflow. Channel acts as the discoverable substrate artifact required by the rule: GHA workflow auto-publishes PR open/merge events and FU register changes on any portfolio repo, with consumerRepo and trackingRepo metadata visible. Promotion path: working-model -> human-loop when interim manual gate exists -> automated when D-COMMS-1 broadcast workflow lands and replaces manual posting.

automated.predicate: D-COMMS-1 GHA broadcast workflow auto-publishes PR open/merge and FU register change events to #luhtech-agents-comms channel with consumerRepo and trackingRepo metadata. Affected chat discovers via channel history read at session start.
automated.ciSurface: D-COMMS-1 GHA workflow on each portfolio repo; publishes to #luhtech-agents-comms Slack channel

refine.action: Branch off main, edit this rule instance, validate against rule.schema.json, PR with rationale.

transitions.trigger: Rule schematized from FU-RULE-CROSS-CHAT-SURFACE-NOTIFICATION-1 (EB register). Origin incident: 2026-06-01 venture-summary archival HALT discovered by EB-agent at execution time, not authoring time. Entry-point promotion: working-model lifecycleStatus + working-model activeTier; terminalTier automated via D-COMMS-1.

notes: Pairs with RULE-PIPELINE-AMENDMENT-SUBSTRATE-RECHECK-1 (same-branch overlap within single repo, automated via FU-D-4 amendment-scope-overlap-check workflow) — this rule extends the same coordination principle to cross-repo cross-chat. Where the paired rule queries deliverable instances in the same repo at PR time, this rule's automated tier (D-COMMS-1) queries the broadcast channel across all portfolio repos at session start. Cross-chat-relay origin: EB-agent surfaced the rule via FU filing on 2026-06-01 alignment prompt; SR chat schematized as instance per cross-chat-relay pattern proven in D-3 (3 of 5 rule instances credit EB-agent contribution). EB-agent credited in authority.author per canonical attribution shape.
